meaning: v.使不快,使(人)生气,冒犯,惹怒
examples: Not wishing to displease her, he avoided answering the question.
We need to know the custom of this country, so that we would not displease anyone.
So go back now; and go peaceably, that you may not displease the lords of the Philistines.
In the circumstances the last thing any government in Europe will want is to be seen to displease the new American president.
But that might displease Blue Capital, as short-term profits would suffer.
